name: James Foley PLLC
description_short: Fort Worth bankruptcy and debt defense attorney specializing in FCRA violations, credit card lawsuits, and Chapter 7 filing. Contingent fee basis for credit reporting cases.
description_long:
James Foley, PLLC is a solo bankruptcy and debt defense law practice based in Fort Worth, Texas, founded by attorney James Foley. The firm focuses on consumer protection and debt-related legal issues across multiple practice areas. The practice serves clients throughout Fort Worth, Tarrant, Parker, Johnson, and Hood Counties, with a stated commitment to putting consumers first. Mr. Foley is registered as a debt relief agency under federal law and offers virtual consultations for initial case assessments.

The firm offers a comprehensive range of debt and credit-related legal services including bankruptcy filing (Chapter 7 focus), credit card lawsuit defense, Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) violation cases, auto loan deficiency defense, credit reporting error remediation, and identity theft victim representation. For FCRA and credit reporting cases, the firm operates on a contingent fee basis, meaning clients pay no attorney fees unless a settlement is reached. The practice also provides educational resources on related topics including debt myths, debt consolidation considerations, bankruptcy alternatives, and credit exemptions.

The firm distinguishes itself through specialized expertise in Fair Credit Reporting Act litigation and consumer reporting agency practices. Mr. Foley emphasizes understanding how credit reporting agencies like TransUnion and Equifax operate, and applies this knowledge in courtroom representation. The contingent fee arrangement for credit reporting cases removes upfront cost barriers for consumers seeking FCRA damages. The website includes educational content designed to help consumers understand their rights and options before litigation becomes necessary.

As a solo practice, the firm's capacity is inherently limited to cases Mr. Foley personally handles. The website contains minimal case results, testimonials, or specific outcome data beyond a reference to Google Reviews. The practice area is narrow—debt and credit-focused—which means it cannot assist with broader legal needs. No information is provided regarding response times, case acceptance criteria, or detailed fee structures beyond the FCRA contingency arrangement.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Operates FCRA cases on contingent fee basis—no attorney fees unless settlement reached
Specializes in Fair Credit Reporting Act violations with stated expertise in credit reporting agency practices
Defends credit card lawsuits and handles old charged-off debt collection defense
Represents identity theft victims and handles merged credit file disputes
Registered debt relief agency offering Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing services
Serves five-county area (Fort Worth, Tarrant, Parker, Johnson, Hood) with virtual consultation options
Provides free educational resources on debt, bankruptcy alternatives, and credit rights
cons: Solo practice with limited capacity—all work handled by single attorney Mr. Foley
Website lacks case results, settlement amounts, or specific outcome documentation to assess track record
Narrow practice focus means cannot address non-debt legal issues or provide broader legal services
No information on typical response times, case acceptance criteria, or fee structure for non-contingency matters
Limited online presence beyond website—minimal reviews or third-party verification of claims
best_for: Consumers being sued over credit card debt or old charged-off accounts seeking legal defense
Individuals with errors on credit reports seeking FCRA violation damages on contingent fee basis
Fort Worth-area residents considering Chapter 7 bankruptcy or needing debt relief guidance
Identity theft victims requiring legal representation to address merged files or fraudulent accounts
diagnosis: Best for consumers in the Fort Worth area facing credit card lawsuits, FCRA violations, or considering bankruptcy who want attorney representation without upfront costs. Primary caveat: This is a solo practice with inherent capacity limitations, and the website provides no verifiable case results or track record documentation—consumers should request references and specific outcome examples before engagement.
services: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) violation litigation
Credit report analysis and dispute assistance
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 4116 W Vickery Blvd, Fort Worth, TX 76107
